exampleSentencesTranslated: If we study hard, we will get good results.
We always try to keep our promises to our friends.
When we are sad, we often listen to music to feel better.
notes:
ta = we (inclusive). It’s a pronoun that includes both the speaker and the listener. It sounds very warm and close, creating a sense of shared belonging.

You often hear it as chúng ta for a more formal or clear plural. In many contexts, especially in signs, rules, or friendly invitations, ta alone is used to mean “our / us”.  

It can also be a poetic or grand way to say “I”, but for A2 level, we focuses on the plural “we/us” meaning which is essential for group talk.
notes_md:
**ta** = “I”, but it sounds poetic, old-fashioned, or very intimate.

It’s a first-person pronoun used in literature, speeches, historical dramas, or when someone wants a serious or emotional tone. In real daily conversation, people usually say **tôi**, **mình**, or **tao** instead.  

It can be used as the subject before a verb, just like other pronouns.  

For memory: **tôi** = neutral “I”, **tao** = rough/casual “I”, **ta** = poetic or grand “I”.
exampleSentences: Nếu ta chăm chỉ học tập, ta sẽ đạt kết quả tốt.
Ta luôn cố gắng giữ lời hứa với bạn bè của mình.
Khi ta buồn, ta thường nghe nhạc để cảm thấy tốt hơn.

example.vi: Ta sẽ đi chơi vào cuối tuần này.
example.es: Nosotros saldremos este fin de semana.
example.en: We will go out this weekend.

definitions.es: Nosotros (incluyendo al hablante y al oyente)
definitions.vi: Chúng ta (bao gồm người nói và người nghe)
definitions.en: We (inclusive of the speaker and the listener)
